Defendants Roger Severin And Doggett-Severin Trusts Notice Of Motion To Strike Claims For Punitive, Exemplary And Treble Damages From Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Betzy Bras-Gonzales, 415.835.6777 for Plaintiff Paul Crug.
John Girarde, 415.788.1900 for Defendant Roger Severin.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS ROGER SEVERIN and DOGGETT-SEVERIN TRUST'S Notice Of Motion To Strike Claims For Punitive, Exemplary And Treble Damages From Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint is DENIED. Mr. Crug alleged sufficient ultimate facts to show that defendants acted with malice and oppression as those terms are defined in Civil Code 3294(c) and to show entitlement to treble damages per the Rent Ordinance. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Betzy Bras-Gonzales, 415.835.6777 for Plaintiff Paul Crug.
John Girarde, 415.788.1900 for Defendant Roger Severin.

Argued by the Defendant. Plaintiff submits.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS ROGER SEVERIN and DOGGETT-SEVERIN TRUST'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to the Eleventh Cause of Action and SUSTAINED with Ten Calendar Days Leave to Amend as to the Twelfth Cause of Action. Defendants' reliance on Stoiber v. Honeychuck (1980) 101 Cal. App. 3d 903, 928 for the proposition that a tenant no longer in possession may not obtain restitution under the UCL lacks merit. In Stoiber the tenant did not seek restitution per the UCL and thus the decision had no occasion to decide whether a tenant not in possession may obtain restitution. ABC International Traders, Inc. v. Matsushita Electric Corp. (1997) 14 Cal 4th 1247, 1269-71 held that a party may obtain restitution per the UCL even if the party did not seek or obtain an injunction and there is no reason to believe that the ABC is inapplicable to a UCL based on unlawful conduct by a landlord. Defendants' argument based on the Health & Safety Code, even if correct, does not support their demurrer since the UCL claim is predicated on alleged unlawful conduct other than that proscribed by the Health & Safety Code. A demurrer lies only to the entirety of a claim. Rather than being supportive of Mr. Crug's assertion that he adequately alleged his constructive eviction claim, Stoiber, 101 Cal. App. 3d at 925-26 shows that the claim is insufficiently alleged. Stoiber says "Abandonment of the premises by the tenant within a reasonable time after the wrongful act of the landlord is essential to enable the tenant to claim a constructive eviction." There are no facts alleged to support this "essential" attribute of constructive eviction. Mr. Crug is granted leave, if he is able to do so, to allege ultimate facts showing that he abandoned the premises within a reasonable time after the condition(s) that made the premises unfit for occupancy. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendants Roger Severin And Doggett-Severin Trusts Notice Of Motion To Strike Claims For Punitive, Exemplary And Treble Damages From Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-24-2021 Continued To Jul-07-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANTS ROGER SEVERIN AND DOGGETT-SEVERIN TRUST'S Notice Of Motion To Strike Claims For Punitive, Exemplary And Treble Damages From Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is continued to July 7, 2021.
